description:
Batch 2/5 — Reactive Scheduling (1 of 2)

Replace the daemon_run.rs main loop (lines 298-464) and the blocking execute_running_workflow_phases_for_project with a reactive coordinator:

Current: loop { project_tick() // blocks on JoinSet; sleep(interval) }
Target: loop { select! { result = pool.next_completion() => process_and_backfill(), _ = housekeeping_timer.tick() => run_housekeeping(), _ = ctrl_c => drain_and_exit() } }

The coordinator:
1. On agent completion: process_agent_result() → advance/rework/fail workflow → call work_queue.next_slot() → pool.try_spawn() if slot available
2. On housekeeping tick: run all non-agent operations (git flush, stale reconciliation, dependency gates, merge gates, binary refresh, promote_backlog, retry_failed)
3. On startup: fill pool to capacity from work queue
4. On ctrl_c: pool.drain() then exit

The pool.next_completion() returns via the mpsc channel from TASK-109. This is where the batch model is eliminated — each agent completion is handled individually and immediately triggers backfill.
title: Replace JoinSet batch wait with reactive pool coordinator loop
